title: Kotlin Type Casting
content:
### Type Casting in Kotlin

Kotlin provides several mechanisms for converting and casting between types.

---

### Explicit Type Conversion

Kotlin does not support implicit widening conversions. Smaller types are not automatically converted to larger types.

```kotlin
val a: Int = 100
// val b: Long = a  // Error: Type mismatch
val b: Long = a.toLong()  // OK
```

---

### Conversion Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `toByte()` | Converts to Byte |
| `toShort()` | Converts to Short |
| `toInt()` | Converts to Int |
| `toLong()` | Converts to Long |
| `toFloat()` | Converts to Float |
| `toDouble()` | Converts to Double |
| `toChar()` | Converts to Char |
| `toString()` | Converts to String |

```kotlin
val intVal = 42
val doubleVal = intVal.toDouble()  // 42.0
val stringVal = intVal.toString()  // "42"
```

---

### Smart Casts

Kotlin automatically casts types after a type check.

```kotlin
fun printLength(obj: Any) {
    if (obj is String) {
        println(obj.length)  // Smart-cast to String
    }
}
```

---

### Unsafe Cast Operator: as

```kotlin
val obj: Any = "Hello"
val str: String = obj as String  // May throw ClassCastException
```

---

### Safe Cast Operator: as?

```kotlin
val obj: Any = 123
val str: String? = obj as? String  // Returns null if cast fails
println(str)  // null
```

---

### Type Checking with is and !is

```kotlin
val value: Any = "Kotlin"
if (value is String) {
    println("It is a string")
}
if (value !is Int) {
    println("Not an integer")
}
```

> **Key Point:** Prefer `as?` over `as` to avoid ClassCastException. Use smart casts whenever possible.

quiz.question: What happens when a safe cast fails?
Which operator performs an unsafe cast?
